text: Alasɔ Ndupiedze Yohanes ɔba ɔ̃ate mi sibɔlɔlɔ ɔri, ɣɛɛ miifɔ wũ miɖe, ɣɛɛ ma lampofɔdze gu asɔrɔ̃maɖedze ɔfɔ wũ maɖe. Atoa sɔ minya nwagbe ne, miifinikira mi adzuni mifɔ wũ miɖe.” Yesu Ɔpɛ Igbã I Marabaradze Lalaa Mawɛ̃ Iso.

ref: The chief priests and leaders answered, “He will kill them in some horrible way. Then he will rent out his vineyard to people who will give him his share of grapes at harvest time.”
text: Matã mmuai sɔ, “Ɔto ɔ̃aɖoe marabaradze lalaa magbe ɔsu kakpɛkɔ̃ ɔtã marabaradze mama ma loasu ɔ̃ kaɖekɔ̃ mabɔ wũ i ɔwi iso si masɔrɛ̃ ɔraɖui.”
translated: Matthew said, "The one who sowed the good seed is the Son of Man, and the field is the world, and the good seeds are the children of the kingdom, but the weeds are the children of the evil one."

ref: The king sent some servants to tell the invited guests to come to the banquet, but the guests refused.
text: Ɔpia ɔ̃ maɖabo sɔ masɛ maakpere mma matã itsue sɔ maba iyi kaɖekɔ̃ sɔ ka maba, ɣɛɛ masɛ̃ sɔ maito maba.
translated: One day I went to the farm to weed, and when I finished weeding and wanted to come back, I saw that it was going to rain, so I came back.

ref: Jesus answered: You are completely wrong! You don't know what the Scriptures teach. And you don't know anything about the power of God.
text: Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Mmɔ miɣɛrɛ ne, alasɔ miiɣe Ɔko Sekelea ame atɔ̃me fiɛ miiɣe Ɣaa ɔle ana.
translated: Jesus answered them, "Truly I tell you, you seek me not because you saw signs but because you ate the loaves and were filled."

ref: They love the best seats at banquets and the front seats in the synagogues.
text: Si makpere ma i ɔpɔrɔ̃ katukɔ̃ ne, masɛ maɖɔɛ kusɛkɔ̃ kɔlɛa. Masɛ maɖɔɛ masɛ i kusɛkɔ̃ sɛɛ gu kusɛkɔ̃ kɔlɛa i ma kusarɛkɔ̃ ana.
translated: If I don't have money in my pocket, I can't buy anything. I can't buy what I want because I don't have money or coins.

ref: But none of you should be called a teacher. You have only one teacher, and all of you are like brothers and sisters.
text: “Madaakpere mi sɔ ratedze, alasɔ mi ɔɖuɖu ne, manyii miɖe fiɛ ratedze ɔwɛ̃ saã miba.
translated: I don't have money to buy clothes, nor do I have food to eat, that's why I came to beg you people to help me.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You're like tombs that have been whitewashed. On the outside they are beautiful, but inside they are full of bones and filth.
text: “Ndzo miɖe mitã mi mmara matedze gu ma Farisise! Mi ma nsɛ mibara ara sɔ si mibo maturi anɔ! Mise lɛ maturi akpibiɔ wa matsue makua kukaakɔ, ɣɛɛ wã ame ɔyi ku makpise buria ku akui.
translated: Woe to you, teachers of the law and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You are like whitewashed tombs, which look beautiful on the outside but on the inside are full of the bones of the dead and everything unclean.

ref: Jesus replied, “Do you see these buildings? They will certainly be torn down! Not one stone will be left in place.” Warning about Trouble (Mark
text: Ne ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Minyɔ ara wagbe ɔɖuɖu kukaakɔ. Alasɔ gbaã nto loɣere mi sɔ mato maabiɛbiɛ wã ɔɖuɖu gɔ ita kuiwɛ̃ iibakuti isia i ne ɔlaa iso sɔ maibasisi mapɛyu.” Yesu Ɔɣere Ɔ̃ Marasuãdze Ɔɣiã Gɔ Maaɖe.
translated: And he said to them, "I have eagerly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer. For I tell you, I will not eat it again until it finds fulfillment in the kingdom of God." Jesus Instituted the Lord's Supper.

ref: But this is just the beginning of troubles.
text: Nwagbe ɔɖuɖu se lɛ kuɣe iɖoɖo ibo ɖeakatɔ̃.
translated: The food preparation took about three hours.

ref: Someone may say, “Here is the Messiah!” or “There he is!” But don't believe it.

translated: If someone comes to you saying, 'Look, here is Christ,' or says to you, 'He is there,' do not believe it.

ref: Two men will be in the same field, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.

translated: When someone dies, before they bury them in the grave, God takes the person's soul or the person's spirit.

ref: You could have at least put my money in the bank, so I could have earned interest on it.”
text: Iyɔ be ɔso fiɛ aisu wũ sikã asɛ aasɛ i sikã kakɔlakɔ̃ sɔ si loba ne, loasɛ loafɔ sɛ̃ ku kusia?’
translated: "If someone's child dies and they bury the child and later the child resurrects and comes back, will they not be very happy?"

text: “Ne makɔse ɔfɔ kanya sɔ, ‘Ɔɖabo lalaa gu dãdze aɖe. Aɣe sɔ nsɛ losu aɖera i ngbegɔ loikpɛ̃ fiɛ nsɛ losa ɔraɖui kanya i ngbegɔ loisa ɔraɖui?
translated: And the priest answered the young man, saying, "The journey on which you go is before the Lord. Is it not that the way you are going and the place you are going to, the Lord knows the place you are going to?"

text: Mita si borui! Minyɔ, miɔnya ngɔ nto ɔɖi me ɔtã.” Ma Yudase Ɔmɔɛ̃ Yesu.

text: to maɣere wũ sɔ, “Gɔ fɔ nɖe Kristo ne, ɣere bo ngɔ lopɛ-ɔ!” Petro Ɔsɛ̃ Sɔ Ɔ̃iɣe Yesu.
translated: They mocked him saying, "If you are the Christ, tell us who hit you!" Peter Denies That He Knows Jesus.

translated: When the servant girl saw Peter again, she said to those standing there, "This man is one of them!"

ref: Peter began to curse and swear, “I don't know that man!” Right then a rooster crowed,

text: Maturi gbodzoo ɔba mmɔ gɔ kukpa kanɛkɔ̃ iisiwe i iyo nɛ kame mapia gu kayogodɔ̃. Ne ɔsɛ i mmɔ ɔɣɛ Itɔ̃me Bielea ne.

text: Matsɛrɛ masɛ i Ɔko Sekelea ame sɔ, ‘Mato maanya, ɣɛɛ maibanya maɖi ite. Mato maanɔ, ɣɛɛ maibanɔ karɔ̃, sɔ si maibafiniki fiɛ Ɣaa aasu ma akpi ɔtsɛ ma.’ ”

text: Ɔrɛrɛ̃ ɔwɛ̃ gɔ marɔ Yairo fiɛ ɔɖe ma Yudase kasarɛkɔ̃ ɔkpakpa ɔba mmɔ. Gɔ ɔnya Yesu ne, ɔpɛ agɛgɛ̃ i ɔ̃ katɔ̃.

translated: After Jesus' death, they took his body and wrapped it in linen cloths. They placed it in a new tomb that had been carved out of rock.

ref: News about him spread all over Syria, and people with every kind of sickness or disease were brought to him. Some of them had a lot of demons in them, others were thought to be crazy, and still others could not walk. But Jesus healed them all.
text: Ɔ̃ iso itɔ̃me ɔki ikã i Siria karɔ̃ ɔɖuɖu iso, ne ɔso maturi ɔsore mma ɔɖuɖu nto manyɛ sinyɛ agbãagbã, mma i so to sibo, mma i siwarã lalaa to siɖaa, mma nto manyɛ kakpebubu gu mma lofɛfɛ̃ marɛ ɔɖuɖu ne, Yesu ɔsa ma ɔɖuɖu.
translated: When he heard that there was a great famine in the land of Syria, and that people were suffering greatly from the famine, and children were dying, and women were crying bitterly, and people were in great distress and there was no help for them from the famine, Jesus had compassion on them.

ref: God blesses those people who are humble. The earth will belong to them!
text: So si siɣɔ mma lobɔ so karɔ̃, alasɔ ma loaɖe kayiiso ara ne.
translated: If you don't greet an elder properly, then he won't give you his blessing.

ref: God blesses those people whose hearts are pure. They will see him!
text: So si siɣɔ mma situ ame lokpa, ala ma loanya Ɣaa ne.
translated: So if you don't want to do bad things to people, then you should fear God.

ref: You are the salt for everyone on earth. But if salt no longer tastes like salt, how can it make food salty? All it is good for is to be thrown out and walked on.
text: “Mi nse lɛ mma mitã maturi ɔɖuɖu, ɣɛɛ si mma ɔyu me ɔmɛrɛ̃ ne, maisibawo ɔri kuwɛ̃ iso ɔki ɔpia me ɔmɛrɛ̃. Misiina kusia kukuwɛ̃ iɖo sɔ maasu me mawiri maturi ɔsɛ me iso.
translated: I know that when we cook food, or when someone is sick, we use medicine from the forest to treat the sickness. We also use wood from the forest to build our houses and other things.

ref: Don't suppose I came to do away with the Law and the Prophets. I did not come to do away with them, but to give them their full meaning.
text: “Loiba lotindza Mose mmara gu Ɣaa kanyamaɖidze ara ite ɖuɖuuɖu, ɣɛɛ loba sɔ si wã ibiara aba i ne ame.
translated: When God gave Moses the law, He forbade them from eating certain foods, but now all things are clean for people.

ref: You know our ancestors were told, “Don't use the Lord's name to make a promise unless you are going to keep it.”
text: “Ɔwiɔ ne minɔ masɛ maɣɛ ana sɔ, ‘Daatu ndamu, Ɣɛɛ bara i nnɛ aɣɛ sɔ aabara atã Ɣaa iso.’
translated: He/She said to me, 'My daughter, God has shown me that he/she will do all God's will.'

ref: You know you have been taught, “An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth.”
text: “Minɔ masɛ maɣɛ sɔ, ‘Si abiɛ fɔ ɔlaa inɔmi ne, maabiɛ fɔ ire, fiɛ si akpa fɔ ɔlaa inyɛ ne, maakpa fɔ ire fɔ wũ.’
translated: If you do not do what I want, I will do it myself, and if you do not give what I ask for, I will give it myself.

ref: If you love only those people who love you, will God reward you for this? Even tax collectors love their friends.
text: Si misɛ miɖɔɛ mma nsɛ maɖɔɛ mi ɔnɔwɛ̃ ne, be kuso miafɔ i Ɣaa kɔrɛ? Ma lampofɔdze ma wũ masɛ mabara ngbɔ.
translated: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would have no sin, but now they have no excuse for their sin. I have shown them my works that I have done among them.

ref: When you give to the poor, don't let anyone know about it.
text: Ne ɔso si abuai wɛrɛba ne, daatã nane fɔ ɔlaa gɔ lomatã-ɔ si ɔtsue nnɛ abara,
translated: When he went to the farm again, he found that all the crops in the farm had dried up,

ref: When you pray, don't be like those show-offs who love to stand up and pray in the synagogues and on the street corners. They do this just to look good. I can assure you that they already have their reward.
text: “Fiɛ si mito mikparama kayi ne, midaase lɛ mma nsɛ mabara ara si mabo maturi anɔ. Masɛ mabie sɔ maata maya makparama kayi i kasɔrɛkɔ̃ gu kuɣa ame sɔ maturi si manya ma. Lobie sɔ lotã mi itsue sɔ ma kuso ame maɔfɔ mmɔ ne.
translated: When we were young, we used to think that when we grew up we would become adults. I thought that when I reached my youth, I would enter into the world of adults and become like them. But now I realize that I am still a child in my heart.

ref: When you pray, go into a room alone and close the door. Pray to your Father in private. He knows what is done in private and will reward you.
text: Ɣɛɛ fɔ si ato akparama kayi ne, kɛlɛ fɔ iyo kame si asɛ̃ iyo apia so fiɛ aakpere fɔ Ɔse gɔ maisɛ manya ala anɔmi, iyɔ gɔ ɔ̃ ɔsɛ ɔnya ta awɛrɛra ne, ɔto ɔ̃atã-ɔ kuso.
translated: If we do not follow the commandments, but instead we do things that are not pleasing to God because we know that He will forgive us when we repent, we are deceiving ourselves.

ref: Come and set up your kingdom, so that everyone on earth will obey you, as you are obeyed in heaven.
text: Fɔ sigara iɖe si siba i bo ndɛ̃. Maabara fɔ kuɖɔɛ i kayiiso lɛ kumɛgɔ masɛ mabara i kato.
translated: You should not smoke cigarettes because it is not good. I beg you to stop it so that you will live long and not die early.

ref: Then others won't know you are going without eating. But your Father sees what is done in private, and he will reward you. Treasures in Heaven (Luke
text: Ne loatã sɔ maturi iibatsue sɔ ato anyi kanya, ɣɛɛ fɔ Ɔse gɔ maisɛ manya, fiɛ ɔsɛ ɔnya ara wɛrɛa aatã-ɔ kuso. Kato Nɖe Ara Ka Kɔlakɔ̃ Sɛɛ.
translated: When a person reaches maturity and becomes an adult, we say God has given them sense, so that they may know how to do their work. This is Called the Age of Responsibility.

ref: But more than anything else, put God's work first and do what he wants. Then the other things will be yours as well.
text: Ɣɛɛ nnɛ iso i mi anɔ si arɛtɛ nɖe sɔ Ɣaa si ɔɖe sigara i mi iso gu si mibara sibɔlɔlɔ ara iyɔ ɔto ɔ̃asu ara wagbe ɔɖuɖu ɔtã mi.
translated: When you do this, you are showing that God has given you a new heart and you have become a new person who does good things every day.

ref: I tell you not to worry about your life. Don't worry about having something to eat, drink, or wear. Isn't life more than food or clothing?
text: Ne ɔso nto loɣere mi sɔ midaamararã so i mi ngbã, nnɛ miaɖe gu nnɛ mianɛ, ɣee mi sosina gu nnɛ miapia iso. Ɛɛbɛrɛ ngbã iifɔ kuɣa miɖo aɖera fiɛ kuturidziri iifɔ kuɣa kuɖo arapia?
translated: If you want to do something and you fail, will you give up or will you try, even if you keep trying and failing? When a child is learning to walk, does he give up the first time he falls?

ref: Why worry about clothes? Look how the wild flowers grow. They don't work hard to make their clothes.
text: “Be ɔso mito mimararã so i nnɛ miapia iso? Minyɔ ipo ame adziri kumɛgɔ asɛ amɔ. Aisɛ ata ɖɔti ɣee awe sikati.
translated: So you think you are better than us? You should know that pride comes before a fall. A tree that stands alone will be broken by the wind.

ref: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father in heaven knows you need all of these.
text: Ara wagbe i maɣɔsɔrɛdze sɛ mabie ne, ɣɛɛ mi Ɔse gɔ i kato ɣe sɔ nwagbe ɔɖuɖu ɔnya mi.
translated: If you don't want to be embarrassed, then God should give you strength so that you can work to get food to eat.

ref: Would you give your child a snake if the child asked for a fish?
text: Ɣee ɔsu aɣɛ ɔtã ɔ̃ ɔbi si ɔkarɛ wũ ikpɛ?
translated: When a person dies, what do they use to bury them in the grave?

ref: But I will tell them, “I will have nothing to do with you! Get out of my sight, you evil people!” Two Builders (Luke
text: Mmɔ loaɣere ma sɔ, ‘Loiɣe mi kukakɔi! Mi ara lalaa mabaradze, mirui me i kɔrɛ.’ Yesu Ɔte Ara I Ayo Matsuedze Agbã Anyɔ Iso.
translated: The man said to them, 'Greetings my friends! You have worked hard, come and eat.' Jesus Showed His Disciples Great Love.

ref: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and winds beat against that house. But it was built on solid rock, and so it did not fall.
text: Kado ɔpɛ, siwore ɔyi, awɛ ɔkpɛ̃ teteree ɣɛɛ iyo iirɔ̃ ala mapia ne karɔ̃ ame kukaakɔ.
translated: So then, the Siwu person, he/she should always keep his/her language so that it does not get lost or die completely.

ref: That evening many people with demons in them were brought to Jesus. And with only a word he forced out the evil spirits and healed everyone who was sick.
text: Ne kutsuɛ ne, makɔ maturi gbodzoo ma i siwarã lalaa si i iso mabɔ Yesu. Ɔsu itɔ̃me iwɛ̃ ɔsa siwarã lalaa ɔɖi i ma ame fiɛ ɔsa mma ɔɖuɖu nto manyɛ.
translated: And when they heard it, they brought all the sick people in their town to where Jesus was. They begged him that the sick people should just touch the edge of his garment.

ref: Jesus went to the home of Peter, where he found that Peter's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
text: Gɔ Yesu ɔsɛ Petro iyo ne, ɔnya sɔ Petro masɔrɔ̃go rɛ, ɔbe ɔtɔ ɔto ɔnyɛ.
translated: When Jesus asked Peter, he knew that Peter would deny him, but he still chose him.

ref: A teacher of the Law of Moses came up to him and said, “Teacher, I'll go anywhere with you!”
text: Mmaratedze ɔwɛ̃ ɔba ɔ̃atu wũ ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Ratedze, loasiai-ɔ kakɔi biara ga aakɛlɛ.”
translated: "The next day when he came to him again he said, "Ratedze, the matter you mentioned yesterday is not possible."

ref: so the disciples went over to him and woke him up. They said, “Lord, wake up! Save us before we drown!”
text: Marasuãdze ɔbore mabo wũ matara wũ sɔ, “Bosate, ɖi bo ne ɔkpi boto!”
translated: The old woman took her child's hand and said, "Please, show him the way!"

ref: a terrible storm suddenly struck the lake, and waves started splashing into their boat. Jesus was sound asleep,
text: Ɔluwɛ̃ ne, awɛ siare awɛ̃ ɔtsɛ ikpɛ̃ i itubu iso gɔ itã ndu to mita mibo i ɔkolo ame, ɣɛɛ Yesu rɛ ɔto ɔrɛ sirɛ.
translated: The crowd was so large that they had to lower the paralyzed man through the roof to where Jesus was standing.

ref: Not far from there a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: Makparako ikuri iwɛ̃ ɣɛ i ndɛ̃ mɛmɔ isɛ mato maɖe ara.
translated: The old woman said that she doesn't have money to buy clothes.

ref: So the demons begged Jesus, “If you force us out, please send us into those pigs!”
text: Siwarã lalaa ɔtã Yesu iti sɔ, “Si aasa bo ne, tã bosɛ boabo i makparako ikuri nɛ nɣɛ i mmɔ ame.”
translated: Then he said to Jesus, "If you want, you can make me clean from this disease."

ref: But Jesus knew what was in their minds, and he said, “Why are you thinking such evil things?
text: Yesu ɔtsue nnɛ mpia i ma adzuni ame ne ɔso ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Be ɔso mito misusu adzuni nyanyarĩa wagbe i mi situ ame?
translated: Jesus asked them, "Who do people say that I am?"

ref: After Jesus had gone indoors, the two blind men came up to him. He asked them, “Do you believe I can make you well?” “Yes, Lord,” they answered.
text: Gɔ Yesu ɔbo iyo ame ne, maba ɔ̃ kɔrɛ, ne ɔkarɛ ma sɔ, “Mifɔ miɖe sɔ loawo mi ɔsa?” Matã mmuai sɔ, “Aĩ, Bosate!”
translated: When Jesus asked them, they answered him, saying, "Do you want us to go and buy bread?" They all replied, "Yes, Master!"

ref: He said to his disciples, “A large crop is in the fields, but there are only a few workers.
text: Ne Ɔso ɔɣere ɔ̃ marasuãdze sɔ, “Ɔraɖui gɔ maasɔrɛ̃ ɔsi, ɣɛɛ marabaradze iisi.
translated: And God said to the man, "You will work hard to get food, until you return to the ground.

ref: Brothers and sisters will betray one another and have each other put to death. Parents will betray their own children, and children will turn against their parents and have them killed.
text: “Ɔwi gɔmɔ ame ne, maturi to maaɖi ma manyii matã sɔ maɖoe fiɛ mase ana to maaɖi ma mabi matã. Mabi to maata maya ma maɣese iso maɖi ma matã maɖoe.
translated: When someone is sick, they should not eat or drink anything cold, so that they don't eat it and then eat or drink something hot. If they eat hot food and then drink something cold, it will harm their body.

ref: Because of me, you will be dragged before rulers and kings to tell them and the Gentiles about your faith.
text: Wũ ɔso ne, mato maamɔɛ̃ mi makɛlɛgu matɔ̃ɖedze gu magara katɔ̃ sɔ maɣɛgu mi atɔ̃me, ɣɛɛ miɣɛ Itɔ̃me Bielea i mma loiɖe ma Yudase katɔ̃.
translated: When he said this, the chief priests and the elders of the people gathered together in the palace of the high priest, whose name was Caiaphas, to plot against Jesus.

ref: But you will not really be the one speaking. The Spirit from your Father will tell you what to say.
text: Alasɔ iiɖe mi loaɖe ika, ɣɛɛ mi Ɔse Siwarã loaɖe ika sikigu i mi iso.
translated: When you go to the farm, you should speak Siwu language with your friends.

ref: Students are not better than their teacher, and slaves are not better than their master.
text: “Rasuãdze kuwɛ̃ iisɛ ɔɖo ɔ̃ ratedze, fiɛ ɔɖabo kuwɛ̃ ana iisɛ ɔɖo ɔ̃ ɔkɔse.
translated: When a man dies and leaves behind a child, then that man has not completely died.

ref: Aren't two sparrows sold for only a penny? But your Father knows when any one of them falls to the ground.
text: Kapɛrɛ kere masɛ marɔdɛ̃ kunagini kunyɔ, ɣɛɛ maisɛ maɖoe kukawɛ̃ si mi Ɔse gɔ i Kato iitɔrã.
translated: The elders said that they would go to the farm tomorrow, but the children should stay at home with Ose and Kato.

ref: Even the hairs on your head are counted.
text: Ɣɛɛ ta mi siwɛrɛ̃ dzɛ i mi ati iso ne, Ɣaa ɣe sɛ̃ kukame.
translated: When you speak Siwu language with your friend, God is very happy.

ref: So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Luke
text: Ne ɔso midaanigã alasɔ mifɔ kuɣa miɖo kunagini. Idaakpɛ̃-ɔ Kunuarɛ Sɔ Aɖe Yesu Ɔre.
translated: And they gave him a piece of broiled fish and he took it and ate it before them. This is the Word of the Lord Jesus Christ.

ref: Don't think I came to bring peace to the earth! I came to bring trouble, not peace.
text: “Midaabu sɔ isobuɛ iɖe losu lobɔ kayiiso. Ooɣo! Iɣɛɣɛso iɖe lobɔ.
translated: The children went to the farm to harvest cassava. Oh no! They harvested a lot.

ref: If you try to save your life, you will lose it. But if you give it up for me, you will surely find it. Rewards (Mark
text: Ɔbiara gɔ loabie sɔ ɔ̃akɔkɔ ɔ̃ ngbã to ɔ̃ayura me, ɣɛɛ ɔbiara gɔ loasu ɔ̃ ngbã ɔtã ala wũ ɔso to ɔ̃ana ngbã. Ɣaa To Ɔ̃atã Ɔbiara Gɔ Lofɔ Yesu Masiaidze Kuso.
translated: Everyone should know that God loves them, but everyone should also know that there is a way to come to God. That Way is Through Jesus Christ Alone.

ref: Anyone who welcomes a prophet, just because that person is a prophet, will be given the same reward as a prophet. Anyone who welcomes a good person, just because that person is good, will be given the same reward as a good person.
text: Ɔbiara gɔ loafɔ Ɣaa kanyaɖidze ne, ɔto ɔ̃ana kaɖekɔ̃ i kanyaɖidze kuso ame. Ngɔ lofɔ ɔturi bɔlɔlɔa ala ɔ̃ sibɔlɔlɔ ɔso ne, ɔto ɔ̃afɔ sibɔlɔlɔ kuso.
translated: Whoever wants to follow God must deny himself and take up his cross. For whoever wants to save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life for my sake will save it.

ref: John the Baptist did not go around eating and drinking, and you said, “That man has a demon in him!”
text: Gɔ Yohanes ɔba ɔ̃ito ɔɖe ara ɔnɛ ndã mɛ loamɔɛ̃ ne, maturi sɔ siwarã lalaa mpia i ɔ̃ ame.
translated: And John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say he has a demon.

ref: You people are like children sitting in the market and shouting to each other,
text: “Kiniɔ ne, be loasu nɔme maturi lokateragu? Mase lɛ mabiɛtɛ ma nto maɣa kaɣa i kuɣa ame. Ma malaa to maɣere ma sɔ,
translated: Now, what should we do about this matter? I say that we should go and meet them. We should go and greet them and see,

ref: But the Son of Man goes around eating and drinking, and you say, “That man eats and drinks too much! He is even a friend of tax collectors and sinners.” Yet Wisdom is shown to be right by what it does. The Unbelieving Towns (Luke
text: Ɣɛɛ gɔ mme, Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi, ɔba fiɛ loto loɖe ara lonɛ ndã ne, maturi to maɣɛ sɔ, ‘Minyɔ ɖeɖei gu tarɛ̃dze gɔgbe! Ɔɖe ma lampofɔdze gu makpibaradze ɔlaa.’ Ɣɛɛ ne, Ɣaa iwola sɛ ite so i ɔturi nsɛgbai ame.” Simagɛ̃ Siwɛ̃ Maturi Iifɔ Yesu Maɖe.
translated: One day, Chief Awune Obi, came in his car and said to my father, 'I want you to come work for me! You will be my lamp carrier and my messenger.' Then, God worked so that the chief would release him." That's How Siwu Fathers Knew About Jesus.

ref: Yes, Father, this is what pleased you.
text: Alasɔ gbaã ne Tete, ngbɔ ikote-ɔ ne.
translated: Alasɔ greeted Tete, but he didn't answer.

ref: Then Jesus told the man, “Hold out your hand.” The man did, and it became as healthy as the other one.
text: Ne Yesu ɔɣere ngɔ kɔrɔ̃ lokarĩ sɔ, “Nabarã fɔ kɔrɔ̃.” Ɔnabarã kɔ̃, ɔluwɛ̃ ne, ilɛ wũ kɔrɔ̃ karĩa ɔse lɛ ngɔ nsarɛ.
translated: When Jesus saw that a crowd was gathering, he said, "Come out of the man." He came out, and the man sat down quietly at Jesus' feet.

ref: When Jesus found out what was happening, he left there and large crowds followed him. He healed all of their sick,
text: Gɔ Yesu ɔnɔ sɔ maɔbara adzuni sɔ maaɖoe wũ ne, ɔrui i mmɔ. Maturi gbodzoo ɔsiai wũ fiɛ ɔsa manyɛse ɔɖuɖu.
translated: When Jesus saw that the crowd was following him, he had compassion on them. He stayed there the whole day and healed their sick people.

ref: but warned them not to tell anyone about him.
text: Ɣɛɛ ɔsɛ̃ ɔtã ma sɔ madaaɖi wũ mate ngɔ ɔɖe.
translated: When a person does something bad, they themselves know it.

ref: “Here is my chosen servant! I love him, and he pleases me. I will give him my Spirit, and he will bring justice to the nations.
text: “Wũ ɔɖabo gɔ loɖi lotã so i ngbe ne. Ɔmɔɛ̃ me ɔtu fiɛ loipia wũ kuɣɛ. Loafedza wũ Siwarã lofere wũ iso sɔ ɔ̃apɛ wũ atɔ̃me iɣɛgu imomo ɔtã abuiti ɔɖuɖu.
translated: The person went to the farm to work there today. The woman cooked food for him to eat. The children of Siwu are learning in school so that they will know many things in the future tomorrow.

ref: If you are not on my side, you are against me. If you don't gather in the harvest with me, you scatter it.
text: “Ngɔ loiɣɛ me i kama ne, ɔta ɔɣɛ me iso, fiɛ ngɔ loito ɔsagu me ara kanya ne, ara ɔto ɔsamarã.
translated: When a person does not want something, they refuse it, but when a person really wants something, they accept it.

ref: Jesus knew what they were thinking, so he said to them: Any kingdom where people fight each other will end up ruined. And a town or family that fights will soon destroy itself.
text: Yesu ɣe ma adzuni, ne ɔso ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Karɔ̃ biara ga maturi loɣɛɣɛ so fiɛ mata maɣɛ so iso aakpese ikpakpi. Ngbɔ ame i ɔmagɛ̃ ɣee iyo biara nɛ maturi loɣɛɣɛ so fiɛ mata maɣɛ so iso iibawo ɔya ne.
translated: Jesus answered them, saying, "Truly, truly I say to you, you are seeking me not because you saw signs, but because you ate of the loaves and were filled.

ref: You are a bunch of evil snakes, so how can you say anything good? Your words show what is in your hearts.
text: Mi ma lonyanyarĩ lɛ maɣɛ! Nda miabara fiɛ miawo ara sɛɛ ɔɣɛ gɔ miɖe maturi lalaa? Alasɔ nnɛ loyi ɔturi ɔtu ame i kanya sɛ kaɣɛ.
translated: I am very happy! But have you forgotten that we should go to the market today? Or is it that you want to stay home with the children?

ref: Some Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses said, “Teacher, we want you to show us a sign from heaven.”
text: Mmara matedze mawɛ̃ gu ma Farisise mawɛ̃ ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Ratedze, boto bobie sɔ bara iwawãra irere si bofɔ-ɔ boɖe.”
translated: The disciples and the Pharisees were fasting, and they came and said to him, "Look, why do they do what is not lawful on the Sabbath?"

ref: Then he taught them many things by using stories. He said: A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
text: Ɔte ma ara gbodzoo i agbã ame. Ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Kpɛ̃dze ɔwɛ̃ ɔsɛ ɔraɖui kasakɔ̃.
translated: He went and found the person at home. He greeted him saying, "Good morning, I have come to greet you."

ref: Everyone who has something will be given more. But people who don't have anything will lose even what little they have.
text: Alasɔ ngɔ mba ara ne, maatã wũ ara ɔ̃ ara ɔsi abua, ɣɛɛ ngɔ ina ne, kɛkɛĩ gɔ ame ɔba ne, maamɔɛ̃ mafɔ wũ iso.
translated: When the time comes, I will gather all of them together, and when that happens, even if someone comes, they won't find anyone at home.

ref: The Son of Man will send out his angels, and they will gather from his kingdom everyone who does wrong or causes others to sin.
text: Mme, Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi, to loapia wũ makpabo sɔ masa mma ɔɖuɖu lokɔ maturi mapia i ikpi ame gu mma lobara ara lalaa kanya.
translated: However, the Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi, has also advised the youth that they should not rely on food from the government alone but should also work hard themselves.

ref: Then he will throw them into a flaming furnace, where people will cry and grit their teeth in pain.
text: Mato maasu ma mafuĩdza mapia i itɔkpe nɛ nto itsue ame ngbegɔ maabiɛ kaku maɖo arɔĩ makpa.
translated: The three men who went to the farm yesterday said they would come back today but they have not yet arrived.

translated: Woe to you, teachers of the law and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You are like whitewashed tombs, which look beautiful on the outside but on the inside are full of the bones of the dead and everything unclean.

ref: After Jesus left the temple, his disciples came over and said, “Look at all these buildings!”
text: Gɔ Yesu ɔbɔrɛ i Ɣaa isɔrɛyo ɔto ɔkɛlɛ ne, ɔ̃ marasuãdze ɔba maakarɛ wũ si ɔkarã inɔ i kumɛgɔ matsue ne ayo iso.
translated: When Jesus finished speaking to the people, a leper came and knelt before him and worshipped him.

ref: Jesus replied, “Do you see these buildings? They will certainly be torn down! Not one stone will be left in place.” Warning about Trouble (Mark
text: Ne ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Minyɔ ara wagbe ɔɖuɖu kukaakɔ. Alasɔ gbaã nto loɣere mi sɔ mato maabiɛbiɛ wã ɔɖuɖu gɔ ita kuiwɛ̃ iibakuti isia i ne ɔlaa iso sɔ maibasisi mapɛyu.” Yesu Ɔɣere Ɔ̃ Marasuãdze Ɔɣiã Gɔ Maaɖe.
translated: And he said to them, "I have eagerly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer. For I tell you, I will not eat it again until it finds fulfillment in the kingdom of God." Jesus Instituted the Lord's Supper.

ref: But this is just the beginning of troubles.
text: Nwagbe ɔɖuɖu se lɛ kuɣe iɖoɖo ibo ɖeakatɔ̃.

translated: If someone had been there at that time to see what was happening, they would have seen the water rising higher and higher until it lifted Noah's ark and it floated.

translated: Why is it that when someone does something bad, people quickly forget? Or when you do something good for someone and later you make a mistake, they will forget all the good things you did for them.

text: Iyɔ be ɔso fiɛ aisu wũ sikã asɛ aasɛ i sikã kakɔlakɔ̃ sɔ si loba ne, loasɛ loafɔ sɛ̃ ku kusia?’
translated: If someone has a hundred sheep and one of them gets lost, won't he leave the ninety-nine on the hills and go to search for the one that is lost?

text: “Ne makɔse ɔfɔ kanya sɔ, ‘Ɔɖabo lalaa gu dãdze aɖe. Aɣe sɔ nsɛ losu aɖera i ngbegɔ loikpɛ̃ fiɛ nsɛ losa ɔraɖui kanya i ngbegɔ loisa ɔraɖui?
translated: Then the elder said to the child, 'A certain man had two sons. Which one do you think did his father's will - the one who said he would go to the farm but didn't go, or the one who said he wouldn't go to the farm but went?'

ref: or visit you while you were sick or in jail?”

text: Mmɔ ɔto ɔɣɛ sɔ, “Ɔwi sɛɛ gɔmɔ ɔɔɣo. Ɣaa sigara ɔɖewi ɔɔbore ɔbo. Mifiniki si mifɔ Itɔ̃me Bielea nɛgbe miɖe.”

text: Maturi gbodzoo ɔba mmɔ gɔ kukpa kanɛkɔ̃ iisiwe i iyo nɛ kame mapia gu kayogodɔ̃. Ne ɔsɛ i mmɔ ɔɣɛ Itɔ̃me Bielea ne.

translated: And when the scribes and Pharisees saw that Jesus was eating with tax collectors and sinners, they asked his disciples, "Why does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?"

ref: No one patches old clothes by sewing on a piece of new cloth. The new piece would shrink and tear a bigger hole.
text: “Kuwɛ̃ iisɛ ɔta ɔkati kuere ala kakatisɛi ɣɛtɛ. Si abara ngbɔ ne, kakatisɛi ɣɛtɛ gamɔ to kaakarĩ karui iyoi nɛ loabɛ iɖo iɖeakatɔ̃.
translated: In the past, when someone died, they would hold a funeral. But nowadays, funerals have become very expensive and difficult for people to organize.

translated: Jesus said that he would come back but he did not say when.

ref: Some teachers of the Law of Moses came from Jerusalem and said, “This man is under the power of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons! He is even forcing out demons with the help of Beelzebul.”
text: Mmara matedze ma lobɔrɛ i Yerusalem ɔɣɛ sɔ, “Siwarã lalaa igara lotã wũ ɔle ɔso ɔto ɔwo siwarã lalaa ɔsa ɔɖi i maturi ame.”
translated: "The law that they wrote in Jerusalem said, 'Every person who kills another person, that person must also die in return.'"

ref: The crowd sitting around Jesus told him, “Your mother and your brothers and sisters are outside and want to see you.”
text: Ne mma lomini wũ i mmɔ ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Nyɔ, fɔ ɔnyi gu manyiibi ɣɛ i inyamɔ mato makpere-ɔ!”
translated: "And the woman said to her husband, "Look, the man of God who always passes by us!"

ref: So if Satan fights against himself, that will be the end of him.
text: Ne ɔso si Ɔbosam sigarakaɖekɔ̃ ɔta siɣɛ so iso fiɛ siɣɛɣɛ so ne, ɔ̃ sigarakaɖekɔ̃ gamɔ iibawo ɔya, siɔba kawirikɔ̃.
translated: When the Devil saw that his cigarette had finished and there was no more left, he asked the cigarette seller for another one, but the seller refused.

ref: But if you speak against the Holy Spirit, you can never be forgiven. That sin will be held against you forever.
text: Ɣɛɛ ne, imusuora biara nɛ i ɔturi aaɣɛ i Siwarã Bielea iso ne, Ɣaa iibasu ɔtsɛ wũ kuiyikuiyi. Maibasu, aɣɛrɛ wagbe matsɛ manya.”
translated: When they heard that the Siwu Bible was going to be launched, God's people rejoiced greatly. They said, "We will see a good thing."

ref: Now listen! A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
text: Ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Kpɛ̃dze ɔwɛ̃ ɔsɛ ɔraɖui kasakɔ̃.
translated: The woman said, "The child is crying because he is hungry."

ref: The reason is, “These people will look and look, but never see. They will listen and listen, but never understand. If they did, they would turn to God and be forgiven.” Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Matthew
text: Matsɛrɛ masɛ i Ɔko Sekelea ame sɔ, ‘Mato maanya, ɣɛɛ maibanya maɖi ite. Mato maanɔ, ɣɛɛ maibanɔ karɔ̃, sɔ si maibafiniki fiɛ Ɣaa aasu ma akpi ɔtsɛ ma.’ ”
translated: The elders said to Oko Sekelea, "We have heard, and we agree with it. We have seen, and we accept it, so that we may finish the work that God has given us to do."

ref: The seeds that fell along the road are the people who hear the message. But Satan soon comes and snatches it away from them.
text: Maturi mawɛ̃ se lɛ ɔraɖui gɔ loɣo i ɔri kuruɛ. Gɔ manɔ ne Ɔbosam ɔmɔɛ̃ ɔɖi i ma iti ame ɔluwɛ̃.
translated: The world has become such that a person goes to the farm with a gun. Because if the Devil's child meets you, he will kill you.

ref: What the farmer is spreading is really the message about the kingdom.
text: Ɔraɖui sadze gɔgbe to ɔsa Ɣaa itɔ̃me.
translated: He/She will go to the farm tomorrow to harvest yams.

ref: Then when harvest season comes and the grain is ripe, the farmer cuts it with a sickle. A Mustard Seed (Matthew
text: Si abi ɔrɛtɛ ne, kpɛ̃dze to ɔ̃asɔrɛ̃, alasɔ ɔraɖui ɔsɔrɛ̃wi ɔɔɣo.” Igbã Isegu Idziribi Kosoi Iso.
translated: If you do this, then you will be a disciple, and you will know the truth." This is what Jesus Christ said.

ref: She had said to herself, “If I can just touch his clothes, I will be healed.”
text: Ala ɔɣere so sɔ, “Si loana ɔ̃ awu kere lopɛgu ne, loto loasarɛ.”
translated: "If you see a snake and kill it, you will be praised."

ref: from the graveyard where he had been living. No one was able to tie the man up anymore, not even with a chain.
text: Ɔrɛrɛ̃ gɔgbe kawekɔ̃ nɖe maturi kabiarakɔ̃. Kuwɛ̃ iisɛ ɔwo wũ ɔnyi ku ala nane ngɔsɔngɔsɔn.
translated: He/She bought a car yesterday but it broke down today. That's why he/she is sitting there looking very sad.

ref: He had often been put in chains and leg irons, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one could control him.
text: Ɔwi gbodzoo si mawo wũ nrɔɔ̃ gu ngba ɔnyi ne, ɔsɛ ɔtu ngɔsɔngɔsɔn gu kugbagɔrɔĩ ɔɖi so. Ɔwe ɔle gɔ kuwɛ̃ iisɛ ɔwo wũ ɔmɔɛ̃.
translated: When a child refuses to listen to advice from an elder, they will face difficulties in life alone. They will then realize that the world is not easy.

ref: Over on the hillside a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: Makparako gbodzoo ɣɛ mato maɖe ara i kabemi kawɛ̃ kuruɛ i mmɔ.
translated: The old woman went to the farm to harvest cassava to cook for her children.

ref: The person in charge of the synagogue was also there. His name was Jairus, and when he saw Jesus, he went over to him. He knelt at Jesus' feet
text: Ɔrɛrɛ̃ ɔwɛ̃ gɔ marɔ Yairo fiɛ ɔɖe ma Yudase kasarɛkɔ̃ ɔkpakpa ɔba mmɔ. Gɔ ɔnya Yesu ne, ɔpɛ agɛgɛ̃ i ɔ̃ katɔ̃.
translated: When he arrived at Jairus's house, he did not allow anyone to enter except Peter, John, and James, and the child's father and mother.

ref: His disciples said to him, “Look at all these people crowding around you! How can you ask who touched you?”
text: Ɔ̃ marasuãdze ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Nyɔ kumɛgɔ i maturi ɔki malɔ bo. Be ɔso fiɛ ato akarɛ sɔ nna lopɛgu-ɔ?”
translated: "He asked the woman saying, "Did God really say you must not eat from any tree in the garden?"

ref: But Jesus turned to see who had touched him.
text: Yesu ɣɛ ɔto ɔpiapia anɔmi sɔ ɔ̃anya ngɔ lopɛgu wũ.
translated: Jesus went to a certain place so that he could pray alone.

ref: The woman knew what had happened to her. So she came trembling with fear and knelt down in front of Jesus. Then she told him the whole story.
text: Ɔrɔ̃go ana ɔtsue kumɛgɔ ka ise wũ. Ne ɔso i ifɔ̃ ame ne, ɔsɛ ɔba ɔ̃apɛ agɛgɛ̃ i Yesu ngbatɔ̃. Ne ɔɖi ɔte wũ kumɛgɔ iba ne.
translated: A man had a son who was sick. When he heard about Jesus, he went and begged him to come and heal his son. And Jesus went with the man to his house.

ref: Jesus heard what they said, and he said to Jairus, “Don't worry. Just have faith!”
text: Yesu iifɔ itɔ̃me ne maɣɛ mmɔ. Ne ɔɣere Yairo sɔ, “Daanigã, fɔɖe kere!”
translated: Jesus took her hand and said to her. Then he called Jairus saying, "Little girl, get up!"

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed the lake and brought the boat to shore near the town of Gennesaret.
text: Gɔ mawara itubu ne, maba maabo Genezaret karɔ̃ iso, ne ɔso mapɛ ma ɔkolo ikpo i ibu katɔ̃.
translated: And when they had crossed over, they came to the land of Gennesaret, and moored to the shore.

ref: But Jesus said, “Prophets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their relatives and their own family.”
text: Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Ɣaa kanyaɖidze ba kuwarɛ i kakɔi biara iɖo ɔ̃ mɔmɔ ɔmagɛ̃, ɔ̃ maturi gu ɔ̃ iyo maturi anɔmi ame.”
translated: Jesus answered them, "Truly I tell you, everyone who commits sin is a slave to sin."

ref: If any place won't welcome you or listen to your message, leave and shake the dust from your feet as a warning to them.”
text: Ɣɛɛ kakɔi biara ga maturi loifɔ mi ɣee maikã mi atsue ne, mikpakpa mi ngba iso sise mifere lɛ Ɣaa kutsue ikpadzɛ̃ nɛ loaba ikparĩ.”
translated: And if any of you lacks wisdom, let him ask of God, who gives to all liberally and without reproach, and it will be given to him.

ref: The girl left and asked her mother, “What do you think I should ask for?” Her mother answered, “The head of John the Baptist!”
text: Ne ɔso, ɔbitɛ gɔgbe ɔsɛ ɔ̃akarɛ ɔ̃ ɔnyi sɔ, “Be si lokarɛ?” Ne ɔ̃ ɔnyi ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Karɛ wũ Ndupiedze Yohanes iti.”
translated: Then, one day he asked his disciples, "Who do people say I am?" And they answered him, "Some say John the Baptist."

ref: The guard put the head on a serving plate and took it to the girl. Then she gave it to her mother.
text: Ɔsu ne ɔsia i kategelei iso, ɔsu ɔba ɔ̃atã ɔbitɛ gɔgbe. Ne ɔfɔ, ɔsu ɔkɛlɛgu ɔ̃ ɔnyi ne.
translated: When a person is sick in bed, the person becomes very weak. And also, the person cannot do anything.

ref: That evening the disciples came to Jesus and said, “This place is like a desert, and it's already late.
text: Gɔ kutsuɛ ɔɣo ne, marasuãdze ɔbore mabo wũ maɣere wũ sɔ, “Kaɖe ɔɔro fiɛ ngbe ɔdzorogu ayo ame.
translated: So when that happened, the Pharisee who had invited him said to himself, "If this man were a prophet, he would know who this woman is."

ref: Jesus replied, “You give them something to eat.” But they asked him, “Don't you know it would take almost a year's wages to buy all of these people something to eat?”
text: Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Mi mɔmɔ mitã ma irere maɖe.” Ne maɣere wũ sɔ, “Nnɛmɔ to iafɔ lɛ ɔturi awa farafana kuso. Ato abie sɔ bonina ngbɔ itã ma aɖera?”
translated: Jesus answered them, "You also ask me one thing." Then he said to them, "John's baptism, did it come from heaven or from men? Or what do you say about it?"

ref: All of them saw him and were terrified. But at this same time he said, “Don't worry! I am Jesus. Don't be afraid.”
text: Manigã gbaã sɔ manya wũ i ndu iso. Ne Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Midaanigã! Mme loɖe. Mina ɔtu.”
translated: The man immediately got up and carried his mat. And Jesus said to him, "My friend! Stand up. Take your mat and go."

ref: At once, Jesus made his disciples get into the boat and start back across to Bethsaida. But he stayed until he had sent the crowds away.
text: Ɔluwɛ̃ Yesu ɔtã ɔ̃ marasuãdze ɔya mabo i ɔkolo ame sɔ mawara makɛlɛ Betsaida. Ne ɔ̃ wũ ka ɔɖi iturikuri ɔri ne.
translated: When Jesus and his disciples came to a village called Bethsaida, some people brought a blind man to him.

ref: Later in the evening he was still there by himself, and the boat was somewhere in the middle of the lake.
text: Si kutsuɛ aaɣo ne, ɔkolo ka ɔsɛ i ibu ndɛ̃, Yesu ɔnɔwɛ̃ ana ɣɛ i ibu katɔ̃.
translated: When they arrived there, instead of the child being dead, Jesus found that the child was only sleeping.

ref: He then got into the boat with them, and the wind died down. The disciples were completely confused.
text: Ne ɔya ɔbo i ɔkolo ame fiɛ ɔwiri ɔnyua ikpɛ̃ ne. Ibara ɔ̃ marasuãdze ɣaa gbaã!
translated: When a person goes to another person's house and sees something bad there. That thing should not be spread around!

ref: In every village or farm or marketplace where Jesus went, the people brought their sick to him. They begged him to let them just touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
text: Ne ɔso ngɔi mɛ pelepele i Yesu ɔki, simagɛ̃ ame o-o, nsɛkɔ̃ o-o ne, maturi ɔsore manyɛse mabɔ kuɣakaɖekɔ̃ fiɛ mapɛ wũ nrɔɔ̃ sɔ ɔtã mapɛgu bidɛ ɔ̃ awu kutsueti kere. Mma ɔɖuɖu lopɛgu wũ ne, mana isosare.
translated: And when they came to Jesus and saw him, that he was not on the cross anymore, but the soldiers had already broken the legs of the two thieves who were crucified with him on both sides. But when they came to him, he was already dead.
